_comment: IsaacLab-Arena Lego composable benchmark (backlog research-isaaclab-arena-lego-001). ASSET-SUBSTITUTION APPLIED: Lego brick assets are not in the local registry (no Lego/DUPLO USD in tool_executor, no Nucleus mount guaranteed in the build-spec environment) — backlog explicitly lists blockers=['isaac_lab_install_required']. Substituted plain-Cube brick-proxies (0.08x0.04x0.024 m flat slabs, ≈ 2x4 Lego proportions, no stud detail). Tags downgraded from isaac:assembly.lego_stack to isaac:assembly.brick_stack. Snap-fit mechanism not modeled — friction-only stack with rubber physics material. Full Lego revival is a V1 follow-on once Nucleus assets are confirmed and apply_assembly_constraint snap joints are validated.
goal: IsaacLab-Arena composable benchmark — brick-proxy stack assembly: a Franka arm picks 4 brick-proxy cubes (0.08x0.04x0.024 m flat slabs, Lego 2x4 proportions WITHOUT stud detail — see _comment for asset substitution rationale) from a source row on the table and stacks them sequentially on a 0.12x0.12 m baseplate. Each subsequent brick rests on top of the previous (layer k drop-z = baseplate_top + brick_thickness*(k+0.5)). setup_pick_place_controller(target_source='curobo') drives the motion with a per-brick drop_targets dict keyed to consecutive z-heights. SUCCESS: ≥3 of 4 bricks delivered above the baseplate xy footprint AND stacked monotonically in z (z_brick_k+1 > z_brick_k by at least 0.018 m). Exercises sequential pick-place with stack-z increment (snap-stack pattern approximation) and tests motion planner against accumulating stack height. Source: github.com/isaac-sim/IsaacLab-Arena composable-benchmark spec.
thoughts:
Design rationale for CP-NEW-isaaclab-arena-lego:

0) ASSET SUBSTITUTION (see _comment field): backlog research-isaaclab-arena-lego-001 declares blockers=['isaac_lab_install_required']. The Lego asset family also requires Nucleus omniverse:// resolution which is not guaranteed in build-spec environments. Substitution rule (verified by grepping the local repo: zero hits on /Lego/, /DUPLO/, /lego_*): replace Lego bricks with plain-Cube brick-proxies sized 0.08x0.04x0.024 m (Lego-2x4 footprint, flat slab without stud detail). Tags use isaac:assembly.brick_stack (NOT lego_stack) and isaac:operation.snap_stack remains because the success criterion (sequential vertical stack with z-increment per layer) is the same shape as Lego snap-fit — only the actual snap-joint physics is unmodeled. Future V1 with Nucleus access can swap brick prim_type='Mesh' with a Lego-brick USD reference and add apply_api_schema(schema_name='PhysxAssemblyConstraintAPI') for true snap fit.

1) Brick-proxy geometry: 0.08x0.04x0.024 m flat cube (Lego 2x4 stud-grid proportional: 2*8mm=16mm but scaled up to 80mm for Franka gripper reach; thickness 0.024 m ≈ 3 Lego plate-heights). USD Cube scale=[0.04, 0.02, 0.012] gives correct extents (USD Cube unit-cube has half-extents). Mass 0.020 kg per brick (light, matches real Lego ~2-3 g but inflated to avoid PhysX numerical noise observed in CP-NEW-brick-stacking with sub-10g mass).

2) Source layout: 4 bricks scattered along table at y=+0.30 m (source-row offset 0.60 m in front of robot). x-positions: [-0.06, 0.06, -0.06, 0.06] paired with two y-rows at +0.26 and +0.34. Each brick centre z = 0.78 (table top) + 0.012 (half-height) = 0.792 m. Bricks oriented identically (no rotation — long axis along x).

3) Baseplate location: 0.12x0.12x0.01 m thin slab at world XY=(0, -0.30), top at z = 0.78 + 0.010 = 0.79 m. Centred behind robot. Drop targets stack vertically:
   - Brick_1: [0, -0.30, 0.802]   (= 0.79 + 0.012 — sits on baseplate)
   - Brick_2: [0, -0.30, 0.826]   (= 0.802 + 0.024)
   - Brick_3: [0, -0.30, 0.850]   (= 0.826 + 0.024)
   - Brick_4: [0, -0.30, 0.874]   (= 0.850 + 0.024)
   Top of final stack ≈ z = 0.886 m, within Franka EE envelope (base at z=0.75, reach ~0.85 m).

4) Robot: Franka Panda on 0.75 m table at world (0, 0, 0.75). Orientation [0.7071068, 0, 0, 0.7071068] = 90° about Z so +X-default forward becomes +Y forward — required because source bricks are at y=+0.30 and baseplate at y=-0.30. Franka reach ~0.85 m horizontal; both source (0.30 m) and destination (0.30 m) well inside reach. Vertical: baseplate top z=0.79; stack-top z=0.886 — Franka EE can reach up to z≈1.05 from a 0.75 m base.

5) cuRobo controller with drop_targets dict: target_source='curobo' (best CPU+GPU collision-aware option per setup_pick_place_controller description). source_paths=[Brick_1..Brick_4] in pick-order; drop_targets dict maps each brick path to its sequential layer drop position. planning_obstacles list includes Table, Baseplate, and any previously-stacked bricks (the canonical author cannot list bricks that don't exist yet — cuRobo's auto-build-from-stage handles previously-stacked bricks at runtime). end_effector_initial_height = max(source_z, top_drop_z) + 0.20 = 0.886 + 0.20 = 1.086 m clears all picks and the growing stack.

6) Snap-fit limitation: real Lego snap-fit involves stud-tube friction-press fit (~1-3 N retention force). This canonical does NOT model snap joints — bricks rest by gravity + table/baseplate friction only. The 'isaac:operation.snap_stack' structural_tag denotes the *intent* (sequential vertical stacking with face-aligned drop targets), not a physically-modeled snap mechanism. Rubber physics material on bricks raises inter-brick friction (μ_static ≈ 1.0) to keep the stack stable through subsequent picks. Failure-mode 'brick tips during snap' captures the reality that this is friction-only.

7) Physics: CPU dynamics (enable_gpu_dynamics=False, broadphase_type=MBP) for deterministic stack settle. solverPositionIterationCount=16 on each brick reduces sub-step explosion when the falling brick lands on the brick below (mass=0.020 kg is light → low impulse, but iteration-count safety margin recommended after CP-NEW-brick-stacking's PhysX numerical explosion). sleepThreshold=0.0 ensures placed bricks stay simulated through subsequent pick cycles (don't auto-sleep mid-task).

8) Mass conservation + monotonic-z verify: count_prims_under_path on the world's brick prims must report n=4 throughout (none deleted/fallen off). get_world_transform on each brick after settle returns translation — verify validates z-positions are monotonically increasing AND at least 3 lie above the baseplate top z=0.79+0.005=0.795. Per-brick z-spacing tolerance 0.005 m (slight settle drop expected as the rubber material compresses minimally).

9) Success threshold: 3 of 4 bricks delivered above baseplate (partial completion acceptable per Arena benchmark spec — Lego assembly is typically per-step-graded, not all-or-nothing). xy_tolerance 0.04 m allows for cuRobo end-of-trajectory placement drift typical of stack-z growth (gripper crab-walks as stack grows).
code:
import json, os

# ── Constants ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
ROBOT_PATH = "/World/Franka"
BASEPLATE_PATH = "/World/Baseplate"
TABLE_PATH = "/World/Table"
GROUND_PATH = "/World/Ground"
SENSOR_PATH = "/World/PickSensor"
# Brick-proxy dimensions (substitution from real Lego asset — see _comment)
BRICK_SCALE = [0.04, 0.02, 0.012]   # half-extents for USD Cube → 0.08 x 0.04 x 0.024 m
BRICK_THICKNESS = 0.024              # used for stack z-increment
BRICK_MASS_KG = 0.020
BASEPLATE_TOP_Z = 0.790              # 0.78 (table top) + 0.010 (baseplate half-z)
RESULT_FILE = "/tmp/isaaclab_arena_lego_result.json"

os.makedirs("/tmp", exist_ok=True)

# ── Scene skeleton ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
create_prim(prim_path="/World/DomeLight", prim_type="DomeLight")
set_attribute(prim_path="/World/DomeLight", attr_name="inputs:intensity", value=1000.0)
create_prim(prim_path=GROUND_PATH, prim_type="Cube", position=[0, 0, -0.5], scale=[20, 20, 1])
apply_api_schema(prim_path=GROUND_PATH, schema_name="PhysicsCollisionAPI")

# ── Physics: CPU dynamics for deterministic stack settle ─────────────────────
set_physics_scene_config(config={"enable_gpu_dynamics": False, "broadphase_type": "MBP"})

# ── Robot table (top at z=0.78) ───────────────────────────────────────────────
create_prim(prim_path=TABLE_PATH, prim_type="Cube", position=[0.0, 0.0, 0.39], scale=[0.8, 0.8, 0.39])
apply_api_schema(prim_path=TABLE_PATH, schema_name="PhysicsCollisionAPI")

# ── Franka arm, rotated 90° about Z (forward → +Y to reach source row) ───────
robot_wizard(
    robot_name="franka_panda",
    dest_path=ROBOT_PATH,
    position=[0.0, 0.0, 0.78],
    orientation=[0.7071068, 0, 0, 0.7071068],
)

# ── Baseplate (thin 0.12 x 0.12 x 0.02 m fixture, BEHIND robot at y=-0.30) ───
# scale=[0.06, 0.06, 0.01] → extents 0.12 x 0.12 x 0.02 m; top at z = 0.78+0.01 = 0.79
create_prim(
    prim_path=BASEPLATE_PATH,
    prim_type="Cube",
    position=[0.0, -0.30, 0.79],
    scale=[0.06, 0.06, 0.01],
)
apply_api_schema(prim_path=BASEPLATE_PATH, schema_name="PhysicsCollisionAPI")

# ── 4 brick-proxy cubes scattered along source row at y≈+0.30 ────────────────
# Brick centre z = table_top(0.78) + half-height(0.012) = 0.792
brick_paths = [f"/World/Brick_{i+1}" for i in range(4)]
brick_source_positions = [
    [-0.08, 0.26, 0.792],
    [ 0.08, 0.26, 0.792],
    [-0.08, 0.34, 0.792],
    [ 0.08, 0.34, 0.792],
]

for path, pos in zip(brick_paths, brick_source_positions):
    create_prim(prim_path=path, prim_type="Cube", position=pos, scale=BRICK_SCALE)
    for api in ("PhysicsRigidBodyAPI", "PhysicsCollisionAPI", "PhysicsMassAPI", "PhysxRigidBodyAPI"):
        apply_api_schema(prim_path=path, schema_name=api)
    set_attribute(prim_path=path, attr_name="physics:mass", value=BRICK_MASS_KG)

# Stay-awake + iteration safety (light bricks survive PhysX numerical edge cases)
bulk_set_attribute(prim_paths=brick_paths, attr="physxRigidBody:sleepThreshold", value=0.0)
bulk_set_attribute(prim_paths=brick_paths, attr="physxRigidBody:solverPositionIterationCount", value=16)
for path in brick_paths:
    apply_physics_material(prim_path=path, material_name="rubber")

# ── Pick sensor near the source row (mid-row at y=+0.30) ─────────────────────
add_proximity_sensor(
    sensor_path=SENSOR_PATH,
    position=[0.0, 0.30, 0.792],
    size=[0.20, 0.20, 0.10],
)

# ── Pre-computed sequential drop_targets (snap-stack layer z-increment) ──────
# Layer k drop z = baseplate_top + brick_thickness * (k + 0.5)
# k=0 → 0.79 + 0.024 * 0.5 = 0.802
# k=1 → 0.802 + 0.024     = 0.826
# k=2 → 0.826 + 0.024     = 0.850
# k=3 → 0.850 + 0.024     = 0.874
drop_targets = {
    "/World/Brick_1": [0.0, -0.30, 0.802],
    "/World/Brick_2": [0.0, -0.30, 0.826],
    "/World/Brick_3": [0.0, -0.30, 0.850],
    "/World/Brick_4": [0.0, -0.30, 0.874],
}

# ── Top-down grasp pose on Brick_1 (template uses gripper_offset=tray pattern) ─
define_grasp_pose(
    robot_path=ROBOT_PATH,
    object_path="/World/Brick_1",
    gripper_offset=[0, 0, 0.04],
    approach_direction=[0, 0, -1],
)

# ── cuRobo pick-place controller ──────────────────────────────────────────────
# planning_obstacles excludes the bricks themselves — cuRobo's stage-auto-build
# picks up already-stacked bricks from the live USD between pick cycles.
setup_pick_place_controller(
    robot_path=ROBOT_PATH,
    target_source="curobo",
    sensor_path=SENSOR_PATH,
    source_paths=brick_paths,
    destination_path=BASEPLATE_PATH,
    drop_targets=drop_targets,
    planning_obstacles=[TABLE_PATH, BASEPLATE_PATH],
    end_effector_initial_height=1.086,
)

# ── Mass-conservation + monotonic-z probes (used by verify) ──────────────────
# Per-brick get_world_transform loop is the mass-conservation signal — counting
# transform-success vs error events gives a precise 4-brick check. An earlier
# revision used count_prims_under_path(parent_path="/World") for this purpose,
# but it overcounted (returned 10+: DomeLight + Ground + Table + Baseplate +
# PickSensor + Franka + 4 bricks) so the intent (verify 4 bricks present) was
# not bound to bricks specifically. The per-brick loop below is the precise check.
for _p in brick_paths:
    get_world_transform(prim_path=_p)

# ── Result-recording stub for the harness ────────────────────────────────────
result_payload = {
    "task": "IsaacLab-Arena-Lego-BrickStack",
    "asset_substitution": "plain_cube_brick_proxy (no Lego stud detail; no snap-joint)",
    "n_bricks": len(brick_paths),
    "drop_targets_z": [v[2] for v in drop_targets.values()],
}
with open(RESULT_FILE, "w") as f:
    json.dump(result_payload, f, indent=2)
print("[RESULT]", result_payload)
extension_notes: IsaacLab-Arena composable-benchmark Lego brick-assembly canonical, with ASSET-SUBSTITUTION applied (plain-Cube brick-proxies, no stud detail) because (a) backlog declares blockers=['isaac_lab_install_required'] and (b) no Lego/DUPLO USD assets are registered locally (grep of /Lego/, /DUPLO/, /lego_* returned zero hits). Structural shape preserved: sequential pick-place with per-layer drop_targets z-increment (snap-stack pattern). Friction-only stack (no apply_assembly_constraint snap joints). V1 follow-on: swap brick prim_type='Mesh' with Nucleus Lego brick USD reference; apply PhysxAssemblyConstraintAPI between stud and tube; rename tags lego_stack and re-baseline.
failure_modes: Brick tips during placement at higher stack layers — Brick_4 drop at z=0.874 lands on Brick_3's 0.04 m wide top face; small cuRobo end-of-trajectory xy drift (±0.01 m) can tilt the upper brick if the gripper releases off-centre. Mitigation: xy_tolerance=0.04 in simulate_args; rubber physics material provides high static friction; partial completion (3 of 4) acceptable.

Brick orientation drift across layers — bricks are placed with default yaw (long axis along x). cuRobo gripper may rotate slightly between picks if IK chooses a different wrist branch — bricks accumulate small yaw rotation up the stack. The 'isaac:operation.snap_stack' tag denotes intent only; this canonical does NOT enforce yaw consistency. V1 follow-on can add gripper_rotation dict to setup_pick_place_controller for explicit per-brick yaw.

ASSET BLOCKER: real Lego brick USD not available locally — substitution to plain Cube brick-proxies was applied at author time (see _comment field). If a future run requires the actual Lego visuals (e.g. for SDG/vision training), swap brick prim_type to 'Mesh' with add_reference(reference_path='omniverse://nucleus/...lego_2x4.usd') and re-validate that Nucleus is mounted in the run environment.

Snap-fit mechanism unmodeled — physics is friction-only (no PhysxAssemblyConstraintAPI between bricks). A real Lego snap fit provides ~1-3 N retention force; here, sufficient lateral acceleration during subsequent picks could dislodge a placed brick. Mitigation: cuRobo motion is interpolated (no jerks), rubber material μ_static ≈ 1.0, and brick mass (0.020 kg) is small so transit-induced inertia is modest. The acceptable failure threshold (≥3 of 4) tolerates one dislodged brick.

PhysX numerical explosion in stack-grip transition — CP-NEW-brick-stacking observed cube velocity blowing to >200 km/s when the gripper grasps a brick with very low mass (<0.010 kg). This canonical uses 0.020 kg per brick and solverPositionIterationCount=16 to stay above the numerical noise floor. If explosion is observed, increase BRICK_MASS_KG to 0.050 or step solverPositionIterationCount to 32.

Franka reach envelope near top of stack — Brick_4 drop at z=0.874 is near the upper edge of the Franka EE envelope from base z=0.78 (reach radius ~0.85 m from base, vertical clearance ~0.95 m). If cuRobo IK fails for the last drop, the controller skips that cycle; partial completion (3 of 4) is still accepted by the success threshold.

Pick sensor too narrow — size=[0.20, 0.20, 0.10] covers the source row (x∈[-0.10, +0.10], y∈[+0.20, +0.40]), but if a brick is nudged outside this bbox by an earlier grasp it will not re-trigger. Mitigation: source bricks are static (kinematic=False but unmoved until picked) so each successive brick remains within the sensor zone.
code_template:
import json, os
ROBOT_PATH = {{primary_robot.path}}
BASEPLATE_PATH = {{assembly_fixture.path}}
TABLE_PATH = "/World/Table"
SENSOR_PATH = {{pick_sensor.path}}
BRICK_SCALE = {{brick_geometry.scale}}
BRICK_THICKNESS = {{brick_geometry.thickness}}
BRICK_MASS_KG = {{brick_geometry.mass_kg}}
RESULT_FILE = "/tmp/isaaclab_arena_lego_result.json"
os.makedirs("/tmp", exist_ok=True)

create_prim(prim_path="/World/DomeLight", prim_type="DomeLight")
set_attribute(prim_path="/World/DomeLight", attr_name="inputs:intensity", value=1000.0)
create_prim(prim_path="/World/Ground", prim_type="Cube", position=[0, 0, -0.5], scale=[20, 20, 1])
apply_api_schema(prim_path="/World/Ground", schema_name="PhysicsCollisionAPI")

set_physics_scene_config(config={"enable_gpu_dynamics": False, "broadphase_type": "MBP"})

create_prim(prim_path=TABLE_PATH, prim_type="Cube", position=[0.0, 0.0, 0.39], scale=[0.8, 0.8, 0.39])
apply_api_schema(prim_path=TABLE_PATH, schema_name="PhysicsCollisionAPI")

robot_wizard(robot_name={{primary_robot.class}}, dest_path={{primary_robot.path}}, position={{primary_robot.position}}, orientation={{primary_robot.orientation}})

create_prim(prim_path={{assembly_fixture.path}}, prim_type="Cube", position={{assembly_fixture.position}}, scale={{assembly_fixture.scale}})
apply_api_schema(prim_path={{assembly_fixture.path}}, schema_name="PhysicsCollisionAPI")

brick_paths = [{{workpieces[0].path}}, {{workpieces[1].path}}, {{workpieces[2].path}}, {{workpieces[3].path}}]
create_prim(prim_path={{workpieces[0].path}}, prim_type="Cube", position={{workpieces[0].position}}, scale=BRICK_SCALE)
for api in ("PhysicsRigidBodyAPI", "PhysicsCollisionAPI", "PhysicsMassAPI", "PhysxRigidBodyAPI"):
    apply_api_schema(prim_path={{workpieces[0].path}}, schema_name=api)
set_attribute(prim_path={{workpieces[0].path}}, attr_name="physics:mass", value=BRICK_MASS_KG)
create_prim(prim_path={{workpieces[1].path}}, prim_type="Cube", position={{workpieces[1].position}}, scale=BRICK_SCALE)
for api in ("PhysicsRigidBodyAPI", "PhysicsCollisionAPI", "PhysicsMassAPI", "PhysxRigidBodyAPI"):
    apply_api_schema(prim_path={{workpieces[1].path}}, schema_name=api)
set_attribute(prim_path={{workpieces[1].path}}, attr_name="physics:mass", value=BRICK_MASS_KG)
create_prim(prim_path={{workpieces[2].path}}, prim_type="Cube", position={{workpieces[2].position}}, scale=BRICK_SCALE)
for api in ("PhysicsRigidBodyAPI", "PhysicsCollisionAPI", "PhysicsMassAPI", "PhysxRigidBodyAPI"):
    apply_api_schema(prim_path={{workpieces[2].path}}, schema_name=api)
set_attribute(prim_path={{workpieces[2].path}}, attr_name="physics:mass", value=BRICK_MASS_KG)
create_prim(prim_path={{workpieces[3].path}}, prim_type="Cube", position={{workpieces[3].position}}, scale=BRICK_SCALE)
for api in ("PhysicsRigidBodyAPI", "PhysicsCollisionAPI", "PhysicsMassAPI", "PhysxRigidBodyAPI"):
    apply_api_schema(prim_path={{workpieces[3].path}}, schema_name=api)
set_attribute(prim_path={{workpieces[3].path}}, attr_name="physics:mass", value=BRICK_MASS_KG)

bulk_set_attribute(prim_paths=brick_paths, attr="physxRigidBody:sleepThreshold", value=0.0)
bulk_set_attribute(prim_paths=brick_paths, attr="physxRigidBody:solverPositionIterationCount", value=16)
for path in brick_paths:
    apply_physics_material(prim_path=path, material_name="rubber")

add_proximity_sensor(sensor_path={{pick_sensor.path}}, position={{pick_sensor.position}}, size={{pick_sensor.size}})

drop_targets = {
    {{workpieces[0].path}}: {{workpieces[0].drop_target}},
    {{workpieces[1].path}}: {{workpieces[1].drop_target}},
    {{workpieces[2].path}}: {{workpieces[2].drop_target}},
    {{workpieces[3].path}}: {{workpieces[3].drop_target}},
}

define_grasp_pose(robot_path={{primary_robot.path}}, object_path={{workpieces[0].path}}, gripper_offset=[0, 0, 0.04], approach_direction=[0, 0, -1])

setup_pick_place_controller(
    robot_path={{primary_robot.path}},
    target_source="curobo",
    sensor_path={{pick_sensor.path}},
    source_paths=brick_paths,
    destination_path={{assembly_fixture.path}},
    drop_targets=drop_targets,
    planning_obstacles=[TABLE_PATH, {{assembly_fixture.path}}],
    end_effector_initial_height=1.086,
)

for _p in brick_paths:
    get_world_transform(prim_path=_p)
verified_status: drafted-2026-05-16-a104; ASSET-SUBSTITUTION applied (plain-Cube brick-proxies in place of Lego — see _comment + extension_notes); pattern_hint=pick_place; destination_kind=fixture; structural_tags include isaac:research.isaaclab_arena + isaac:assembly.brick_stack + isaac:operation.snap_stack; form-gate ⏳; function-gate ⏳ (requires runtime stack-z monotonicity check after sim settles)
